title: Derek Smith- Aug 2025 webinar - ITDR
quoteText: "ITDR turns identity into a living monitored signal—not just a credential."
quoteSource: —Dr. Derek A. Smith, HART Technical Program Manager/Deputy Director at U.S. Department of Homeland Security

title: Derek Smith- Aug 2025 webinar - eliminating security gaps
quoteText: "If [identity] systems operate in silos, your security posture will always have gaps. What we need is unified identity governance. That means every identity, human and machine, goes to a standardized life cycle. Every permission is requested, approved, and reviewed consistently, and every access decision is made in real time based on policy and context."
quoteSource: —Dr. Derek A. Smith, HART Technical Program Manager/Deputy Director at U.S. Department of Homeland Security

title: Derek Smith- Aug 2025 webinar - zero trust
quoteText: "Zero trust is more than just a buzzword. It's a mindset, and identity is what brings it to life."
quoteSource: —Dr. Derek A. Smith, HART Technical Program Manager/Deputy Director at U.S. Department of Homeland Security

title: Derek Smith- Aug 2025 webinar - machine identities
quoteText: "[Machine] identities become permanent backdoors—not because of bad intent, but because of neglect. Attackers know how to exploit this. They search for forgotten accounts or unused tokens. And once they find one, they move freely because nobody's watching them."
quoteSource: —Dr. Derek A. Smith, HART Technical Program Manager/Deputy Director at U.S. Department of Homeland Security

title: Derek Smith- Aug 2025 webinar - identity control plane
quoteText: "We have to stop anchoring our strategies to the ideas of trusted networks. Instead, we need to focus on trusted identities. We have to monitor behavior after login, not just enforcing multifactor authentication before they log in. And most importantly, we have to treat identity, not as a convenience, but as a control plane."
quoteSource: —Dr. Derek A. Smith, HART Technical Program Manager/Deputy Director at U.S. Department of Homeland Security

title: Derek Smith- Aug 2025 webinar - identity visibility
quoteText: "It's about knowing every identity that touches your environment, human or machine. It's about understanding, seeing not just what they're allowed to do, but what they're actually doing. And it's about control—giving access in real time based on risk, policy, and intent."
quoteSource: —Dr. Derek A. Smith, HART Technical Program Manager/Deputy Director at U.S. Department of Homeland Security

title: Derek Smith- Aug 2025 webinar - log in not break in
quoteText: Most of the breaches that we investigate didn't happen because somebody hacked into the system. They happened because someone logged in. The attacker didn't break the system; they used a valid credential. They bypass your perimeter entirely. And why does this happen? Probably because the system trusted the identity.
quoteSource: —Dr. Derek A. Smith, HART Technical Program Manager/Deputy Director at U.S. Department of Homeland Security

quoteText: En la era de la seguridad de identidades, hay una enorme superficie de ataque para que los atacantes consigan un espacio en su entorno. Pueden comenzar localmente y terminar en la nube; la identidad de un usuario puede ser comprometida y utilizada para acceder a datos en una aplicación SaaS sin nunca tocar su red; o un soporte técnico puede ser manipulado para eludir la autenticación multifactor.

quoteText: Más que nunca, los atacantes están aprovechando las malas configuraciones y manipulando la lógica de la aplicación para saltarse etapas de autenticación y otras medidas de seguridad. quoteText: Muchas empresas todavía son reactivas en cuanto a la ciberseguridad, respondiendo solo después de un ataque. Una ciberseguridad fuerte no se trata solo de tener más herramientas, sino de cómo utilizarlas de manera efectiva en una estrategia bien coordinada que evoluciona con las nuevas amenazas.
quoteSource: —Paula Januszkiewicz, CEO y Propietaria, CQURE

title: Identity Security Defense in Depth Guide - Identity Siloes
quoteText: "In large organizations, identity systems are typically managed by separate teams spread across IT and engineering—each with their own reporting structures, focuses, and goals. The proliferation of different identity silos creates hidden risks within and between these systems, giving rise to attack pathways that can traverse from one silo to the next."
quoteSource: —The Guide to Identity Security Defense-in-Depth, BeyondTrust

title: Identity Security Defense in Depth Guide - Human Error
quoteText: "Managing identities across a vast expanse of corporate systems is complex. Consequently, identity security is particularly prone to human error and poor security hardening and hygiene practices."
quoteSource: —The Guide to Identity Security Defense-in-Depth, BeyondTrust

title: Marc Maiffret - MVR 2025 - fundamentals of identity security
quoteText: “The Microsoft Vulnerabilities report has shown time and time again that attackers rely on exploiting privileges alongside vulnerabilities to achieve their goals. Ultimately, patching known vulnerabilities and applying least privilege to identities are fundamental to reducing your attack surface and lessening the impact of a breach.”
quoteSource: —Marc Maiffret, CTO, BeyondTrust

title: Chuck Brooks - MVR 2025 - Zero Trust
quoteText: “Zero trust is anchored in an identity and access focus, and centers defenses on users, assets, and resources rather than static, network-based perimeters. That is our future verification of all people, devices in the network, and knowing and controlling who should have access to be part of it.”
quoteSource: —Chuck Brooks, Subject Matter Expert, DHS CISA Space Systems Critical Infrastructure, US Department of Homeland Security

title: Charles Henderson - MVR 2025 - defense-in-depth
quoteText: “Although it isn’t possible to keep 100% of threats out, taking a posture of assumed breach will still go a long way to driving meaningful security outcomes. Remember, defense-in-depth is crucial.”
quoteSource: —Charles Henderson, VP of Cyber Security Services, Coalfire, Former Head of X-Force at IBM

title: Sami Laiho - MVR 2025 - Windows admin risk
quoteText: “There is no security in Windows if you log in as an admin. All operating systems are the same in this sense.”
quoteSource: —Sami Laiho, Senior Technical Fellow, Microsoft MVP

title: Sami Laiho - MVR 2025 - proactive identity security
quoteText: “If we consider that most identity-targeted attacks will target the routes that people use for online services, the rise in the number of vulnerabilities in Edge and Office is concerning. The good thing is that proactive measures will help you. 40% of vulnerabilities found aim at getting admin or SYSTEM-level access to the operating system—what we should not give the attacker.”
quoteSource: —Sami Laiho, Senior Technical Fellow, Microsoft MVP

title: Kip Boyle - MVR 2025 - prioritizing PAM
quoteText: “Organizations must prioritize privileged access management, implementing time-limited access, requiring robust authentication for elevation requests, and maintaining detailed logs of all privileged activities. Uncontrolled administrative access isn't just a security risk; it's a business liability.”
quoteSource: —Kip Boyle, CISO, Cyber Risk Opportunities LLC

title: Kip Boyle - MVR 2025 - elevated privileges risks
quoteText: “Elevated privileges give attackers access to your software deployment infrastructure. This allows them to distribute ransomware throughout your network using your own trusted distribution channels, making detection extremely difficult. It's like giving thieves the keys to your house and watching them use your own moving company to steal your belongings.”
quoteSource: —Kip Boyle, CISO, Cyber Risk Opportunities LLC

title: Henrik Parkkinen - MVR 2025 - security context
quoteText: “When it comes to security, context is king. A security risk doesn’t operate in a vacuum. It exists in a context. It’s a part of your organization. And there is actually no such thing as cybersecurity risks, it is rather a category of risk. The only type of risk that exists is business/organizational risk.”
quoteSource: —Henrik Parkkinen, Cybersecurity Leader

title: Henrik Parkkinen - MVR 2025 - privileged access
quoteText: “The latest numbers in the Microsoft Vulnerabilities Report don’t lie—a huge amount of Remote Code and Elevation of Privilege vulnerabilities are leading the way once again. Managing privileged access by implementing least privilege security principles, as well as striving for total visibility across your IT landscape, are key to mitigate these risks at-source.”
quoteSource: —Henrik Parkkinen, Cybersecurity Leader

title: Henrik Parkkinen - MVR 2025 - security hygiene
quoteText: "Security hygiene will never go out of fashion. To ensure current and future readiness, I’m a strong believer in that the things which fall into the category of security hygiene need to be prioritized today, tomorrow, and in a continuum."
quoteSource: —Henrik Parkkinen, Cybersecurity Leader

title: Anton Chuvakin - MVR 2025 - overprivileged user accounts
quoteText: "Overprivileged user accounts are the low hanging fruit attackers are looking for, and they will always take the path of least resistance. It’s crucial to secure your privileged accounts (and all pathways to them) to avoid sneaky attack vectors and lateral movement through your network."
quoteSource: —Anton Chuvakin, Security Advisor at Office of the CISO, Google Cloud

title: Paula Januszkiewicz - MVR 2025 - proactive security
quoteText: "Many businesses still rely on reactive security, only responding after an attack happens. Instead, they should focus on constantly monitoring for threats, using advanced analytics, AI-driven detection, and red-teaming exercises. Strong cybersecurity isn’t just about having more tools—it’s about using them effectively in a well-coordinated security strategy that evolves alongside new threats."
quoteSource: —Paula Januszkiewicz, CEO & Owner, CQURE

title: Charles Henderson - MVR 2025 - proactive security
quoteText: "The most successful organizations acknowledge that perfection is impossible and build their security programs around the assumption that the threats already have initial access."
quoteSource: —Charles Henderson, VP of Cyber Security Services, Coalfire, Former Head of X-Force at IBM

title: Anton Chuvakin - MVR 2025 Quote - Patching
quoteText: "Patching is important, sure. So is patching fast. But it's not a silver bullet, it's not even a copper bullet. It’s useful, but you'll need a whole toolbox of other stuff. If your entire security strategy hinges on 'patch all the things ASAP,' you're going to have a bad time. Think least privilege, think segmentation, zero trust, think 'what if we don't patch?'"
quoteSource: —Anton Chuvakin, Security Advisor at Office of the CISO, Google Cloud

title: Marc Maiffret - MVR 2025 - identity security
quoteText: "In the age of identity security, there is more attack surface than ever for attackers to gain a foothold in your environment. Attacks can start on-prem, but end in the cloud; a user’s identity can be compromised and used to access data in a SaaS app without ever touching your network; or a help desk can be socially engineered to bypass MFA."
quoteSource: —Marc Maiffret, CTO, BeyondTrust

title: Anton Chuvakin - MVR 2025 - defense in depth
quoteText: "More than ever, attackers are leveraging misconfigurations and manipulating application logic to skip past authentication steps and other security measures. This is why we need a multi-layered strategy to fortify every step of security. Defense in depth works!"
quoteSource: —Anton Chuvakin, Security Advisor at Office of the CISO, Google Cloud
